source_packet.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
source_packet.audience: Readers who understand dot-product attention and want a geometric account of positional encoding.
source_packet.central_question: How can absolute token positions enter query and key vectors while attention scores depend only on relative displacement?

media.caption: A Manim cascade from paired coordinates to relative phase in the attention dot product.
Poster frame for the RoPE cascade.
English captions for the RoPE cascade.

paper_excerpts.text: The paper positions RoPE after learned and sinusoidal absolute encodings and prior relative-position methods. Its target is to preserve an absolute representation in each vector while making the inner product encode relative position.
RoPE applies block-diagonal two-dimensional rotations to query and key coordinates. Orthogonality turns their inner product into a rotation by the position difference m-n.
On WMT 2014 English-to-German under the paper's setup, Table 1 reports BLEU 27.5 for RoFormer and 27.3 for the Transformer-base baseline.

code_notes.text: The official pseudocode expands cosine and sine values across paired dimensions, constructs a quarter-turn companion for q and k, applies q cos + q⊥ sin and k cos + k⊥ sin, then takes the standard attention dot product.

agent_input.claim_policy: Use the paper's equations and table values with locators. Treat geometric narration as an interpretation of the derivation, and do not generalize the reported BLEU result beyond its stated setup.

concept_graph.thesis: Rotating paired query and key coordinates by their absolute positions makes their dot product depend on relative displacement.

nodes.label: Learned absolute positions
nodes.summary: Adds a learned position vector to each token representation.

nodes.label: Sinusoidal positions
nodes.summary: Uses fixed frequencies to encode each absolute index.

nodes.label: Relative-position attention
nodes.summary: Places relative displacement directly into the attention calculation.

nodes.label: Paired 2D rotations
nodes.summary: Treats each adjacent feature pair as a plane and rotates it by a frequency-scaled position.

nodes.label: Relative-phase dot product
nodes.summary: Orthogonality reduces two absolute rotations to one rotation by n-m inside the score.

edges.teaching_point: Both attach a position identity before attention.
Attention often needs distance and direction, not only absolute identity.
Sinusoidal structure becomes an operation on q and k.
RoPE realizes relative position through vector geometry.
The score retains only the phase difference.

unresolved: The paper motivates long-term decay through the frequency mixture, but downstream length extrapolation behavior depends on model and training conditions beyond this derivation.

lesson_plan.title: Turn position into relative phase
lesson_plan.promise: Start from what attention needs, build one two-dimensional rotation, lift it to all feature pairs, then inspect the exact code and reported result.

sections.title: A score needs distance and direction
sections.question: Why is adding an absolute position vector not the whole story?
sections.learning_goal: State the desired relative-position property of the attention score.
sections.misconception: Token order is already implicit in the dot product.
sections.summary: Without a positional operation, permuting tokens does not give attention a notion of displacement.

sections.title: Absolute codes, relative biases, rotary geometry
sections.question: What does RoPE preserve and what does it change?
sections.learning_goal: Compare learned absolute, sinusoidal, and relative-position predecessors.
sections.misconception: RoPE is simply another vector added to token embeddings.
sections.summary: RoPE turns position from additive content into a rotation applied inside attention.

sections.title: One feature pair is a plane
sections.question: What does a position-dependent rotation do geometrically?
sections.learning_goal: Manipulate q and k as paired 2D vectors with position-scaled angles.
sections.misconception: The whole hidden vector rotates with one shared frequency.
sections.summary: Each adjacent pair rotates in its own frequency band.

sections.title: Two absolute angles collapse to one difference
sections.question: Why does the dot product retain n-m?
sections.learning_goal: Follow orthogonality from Rm and Rn to R(n-m).
sections.misconception: RoPE removes absolute position before attention.
sections.summary: Absolute rotations remain on q and k, but their inner product depends on relative phase.

sections.title: The quarter-turn trick
sections.question: How do slices implement a 2D rotation?
sections.learning_goal: Map paired-coordinate algebra to the official pseudocode.
sections.misconception: A dense rotation matrix must be materialized.
sections.summary: Negate-and-swap constructs the perpendicular vector, then cosine and sine combine it with the original.

sections.title: Evidence in the paper's scope
sections.question: What does the reported WMT comparison establish?
sections.learning_goal: Read a small exact effect without inflating its scope.
sections.misconception: A 0.2 BLEU difference establishes all claimed properties.
sections.summary: The table is one controlled task result; other experiments and the derivation support different parts of the story.

sections.title: Geometry is not a universal guarantee
sections.question: What follows from the identity, and what still depends on training?
sections.learning_goal: Separate the exact algebraic property from empirical extrapolation claims.
sections.misconception: Relative phase alone guarantees arbitrary-context generalization.
sections.summary: The identity is exact; performance and length behavior remain conditional on architecture, frequencies, data, and training.

blocks.heading: Give each vector an absolute phase, but let the score see a difference
paragraphs: Attention compares query and key vectors. The desired positional construction should preserve token position in those vectors while making their inner product respond to displacement and direction.

columns.label: Absolute identity
columns.question: Where is this token?
columns.answer: Encode m into q and n into k.

columns.label: Relative relation
columns.question: How far apart are they?
columns.answer: Make the score depend on n−m.

blocks.prompt: If q and k receive the same extra rotation, should their dot product change?
blocks.answer: No. A shared orthogonal rotation preserves their angle and dot product.

nodes.label: RoPE
nodes.note: rotate q and k by position

blocks.heading: What changes
paragraphs: RoPE borrows sinusoidal frequency structure but changes its role: position is no longer only an additive feature. It becomes an orthogonal transformation inside attention.

blocks.title: One adjacent feature pair
blocks.note: Every pair i uses its own frequency θi; the visual isolates one plane.

blocks.heading: Why pair coordinates
paragraphs: A two-dimensional plane is the smallest space with a continuous rotation. RoPE tiles the hidden dimension with these planes, so different pairs carry different angular frequencies.

blocks.title: Bottom-up animation cascade
blocks.caption: Coordinate pair → two rotations → relative phase → dot product

steps.label: Rotate q
steps.meaning: Absolute position m becomes query phase.

steps.label: Rotate k
steps.meaning: Absolute position n becomes key phase.

steps.label: Cancel shared phase
steps.meaning: Orthogonality converts two absolute rotations into one relative rotation.

blocks.heading: No dense matrix required
paragraphs: The negate-and-swap slice creates a 90-degree companion vector. Elementwise cosine and sine then implement every 2D block rotation in parallel.

blocks.title: WMT 2014 English→German
blocks.claim_label: reported exact values; truncated axis shown

blocks.heading: Read the bar narrowly
paragraphs: Under the paper's stated WMT setup, RoFormer reports a 0.2 BLEU increase. This supports competitiveness on that task; it does not by itself establish the geometric identity or universal length generalization.

items.label: Exact
items.detail: The relative-phase identity follows algebraically from orthogonal rotations.

items.label: Conditional
items.detail: Task performance depends on architecture, training data, optimization, and the chosen frequency schedule.

items.label: Length behavior
items.detail: Long-term decay is motivated by the frequency mixture, but extrapolation is not guaranteed by the identity alone.

items.label: Implementation scope
items.detail: The official README excerpt is pseudocode; production kernels may reorder or fuse the same operations.

blocks.prompt: Does R(n−m) mean q and k lost their absolute positions?
blocks.answer: No. q and k are individually rotated by m and n. Only their dot product reduces those phases to a difference.

appendix_entries.title: Source provenance
appendix_entries.body: Paper arXiv:2104.09864v5, 14 pages, PDF SHA-256 e9a481…af79. Official RoFormer repository revision dfc678ad…a9b4. Media are hash checked during validation.

appendix_entries.title: Related-work map
appendix_entries.body: The explainer distinguishes learned absolute encodings, sinusoidal absolute encodings, earlier relative-position attention, and RoPE's rotation-based construction. This is a conceptual lineage, not a priority claim.

appendix_entries.title: Block-diagonal rotation
appendix_entries.body: Equation 15 stacks 2×2 rotation blocks. Each coordinate pair has angle mθi, allowing the hidden vector to carry multiple positional frequencies.

appendix_entries.title: Formula-to-animation mapping
appendix_entries.body: Axes and vector transforms are built-in Manim primitives. The relative-rotation cancellation is a newly authored composite. The registry preserves candidate versus unresolved mappings rather than claiming exact automation coverage.

appendix_entries.title: Code mapping
appendix_entries.body: The committed documentation snapshot preserves the official README pseudocode and upstream commit identity. Negate-and-swap maps to a quarter turn; cosine and sine form the rotation.

appendix_entries.title: Results reading guide
appendix_entries.body: The WMT bar uses exact Table 1 values and explicitly marks its truncated axis. The text limits the conclusion to the reported setup.

appendix_entries.title: Limits of the geometric story
appendix_entries.body: The algebra establishes relative phase inside the score. It does not alone determine optimization quality, downstream performance, or arbitrary-context extrapolation.
